question: What is the purpose of integrating Basic Engineering Mathematics into the BSECE program?
contexts:
['The BSECE program, with Ignatian ideals, emphasizes core competencies that are essential for ECE
Braduates. These competencies reflect technical skills and Ignatian values such as problem-solving, design skills,
critical thinking, and more. To ensure that these competencies are attained, it is essential to strengthen foundational
Subjects like mathematics. This will be achieved by integrating Basic Engineering Mathematics into the program.

Adding Basic Engineering Mathematics will allow STEM and non-STEM SHS to Join the program. As an option,
emerging technology professional electives are included with the demand for new technology. The new professional
electives courses will supplement the senior’s projects. In addition to the revision, the course code for Differential
Equation and Engineering Technopreneurship was changed to align with other engineering programs.

a. Program Title and Description']
ground_truth: to strengthen foundational subjects like mathematics and ensure core competencies are attained

question: What disciplines are the Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering program grounded in?
contexts:
['a. Program Title and Description

The Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering (BSECE) program is structured to cultivate engineers with a
robust grounding in mathematics, natural sciences, physical sciences, and related disciplines. Accordingly, the
program comprises mathematics, science, and engineering fundamentals courses, focusing on nurturing analytical
and creative skills. It also encompasses language studies, social sciences, humanities, and other core courses to
ensure that graduates are articulate and comprehend the significance of their roles in society and the impact of their
work on societal advancement.']
ground_truth: mathematics, natural sciences, physical sciences, and related disciplines

question: What is the Average Quality Point Index for the given example?
contexts:
["3. Computation of Average Quality Point Index

The Average Quality Point Index (QP!) is used as a measure of student's academic performance. To
determine the average OPI, the letter grades are given point values as shown in the Transmutation Table for
the 2018 Revised Grading System. It is computed as the quotient of the Accumulated Grade (AG) and the
Total Units (TU). An example showing the procedure is shown below:

; . Letter | Point Value .
CSDC100 — Introduction to Computing | 3 | B | 30 | 900 |
CSDC101 — Fundamentals of Programming | 3 | B | 30 | 900 |
\THS101 = Mathematics in the Modern World
Average OP! = AG/TU = 62.50/20 = 3.125

question: What is the deadline for completing delayed final examinations?
contexts:
["1.1 Delayed preliminary, midterm and pre-final examinations must be completed no later
than the last day prior to the schedule of the next major exam period (e.g., delayed
preliminary exams must be taken not later than the last recitation or class day before
the midterm exams).
1.2 Delayed final examinations must be completed no later than the first week of classes
of the succeeding term, summer sessions included.
1.3 Applications for delayed preliminary, midterm and pre-final examinations must be filed
no later than a week before the next scheduled examinations.
1.4 Aconcerned student may appeal a disapproved application for delayed examination
to the Dean of his/her Home College.
1.5 The College Registrar's Office shall announce and/or publish the approved deadlines
for filing of applications and the delayed final examinations.
2. Valid reasons for delayed examinations and supporting documents. The following are"]
ground_truth: no later than the first week of classes of the succeeding term, summer sessions included

question: What is the maximum number of units a student on academic probation can enroll in for the next semester?
contexts:
["Academic Regulations = s
I. Academic Probation

A student who obtains a OPI below 1.5, in all subjects for the semester is placed on Academic
Probation in the next semester. A student already on academic probation who still merits a succeeding
Academic Probation is not allowed to enroll in more than 18.5 units (PE and NSTP included) in the next
semester. Moreover, the Department Chairperson in consultation with the student's counselor regulates
the subject load of said student.

J. College Reading Program

All incoming college freshman students and transferees of Ateneo de Naga University are re-
quired to take the College Reading Program except those who had finished a bachelor's degree prior
to enrollment of their present course. Incoming college freshman students who finished senior high
school in the University and were issued a Certificate of Proficiency are also exempted from taking
the College Reading Program."]

question: What is the duration for which a student will be placed on Strict Disciplinary Probation Status after receiving a penalty short of dismissal
contexts:
['d. Malversation of funds collected from the students
e. Misrepresentation in the application for school-sponsored awards (e.g. graduation awards) or
school representation/nomination to external searches/competitions/programs
f. Any other forms of dishonesty
6. Offenses against Public Morals
a. Accessing, possessing or distributing pornographic materials on campus, whether in physical
or electronic form
b. Engaging in, or provoking, scandalous behavior on campus or during a school-sanctioned function
c. Any form of public immorality
When the penalty imposed on a student is short of dismissal, i.e., suspension and/or reprimand,
the student concerned will be placed on “Strict Disciplinary Probation Status” (SDPS) for not less than
one semester but not more than one academic year.
* Exception: Sharing private communications is permitted only when done for legitimate purposes,
including but not limited to:']
ground_truth: not less than one semester but not more than one academic year

question: What happens to a student's credentials during the period of suspension?
contexts:
["r i (re CHAPTER 7

2. Suspension. MoRPHE's Suspension deprives or denies the erring student from attending his/

her in classes and school activities for a definite period of time during a semester. The student
shall be reinstated in the school after the duration of suspension and upon compliance with the
sanctions and interventions as stated in the decision. During the period of suspension, the erring
student's credentials are withheld.
A student under investigation of a case involving the penalty of suspension, dismissal or expul-
sion may be preventively suspended from entering the school premises if the evidence of guilt is
strong and/or the school head is morally convinced that the continued stay of the student during
the period of investigation constitutes sufficient distraction to the normal operation of the school
or poses real or imminent threat or danger to persons and property in the school's premises and
activities."]
